text.content:
⚓ Oblivion

Level 1: Shadow Cloak

Cost: Free | Pool: -- | Type: Utility

Wrap yourself in a layer of living shadow. Grants +2 dice to Stealth rolls in darkness. In dim light, you appear as a deeper shadow — unsettling but not obviously supernatural. The shadow cloak also dampens sounds slightly. If examined closely in bright light, the shadows are visibly unnatural. Lasts one scene.

Level 1: Oblivion's Sight

Cost: Free | Pool: Wits + Oblivion | Type: Passive

Peer through the Shroud into the Shadowlands. You see ghostly echoes of the dead overlaid on reality — spirits, haunts, and remnants of death. You can identify recently deceased individuals, sense how someone died at a location, and see ghosts that are present but invisible. While active, the living world appears dimmer and the dead brighter. You can communicate with visible ghosts but cannot physically interact with them. Lasts one scene.

Level 1: Ashes to Ashes

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: -- | Type: Utility

Touch a corpse (mortal only) to reduce it to fine ash in seconds. The ash is inert and indistinguishable from wood ash. All forensic evidence is destroyed — dental records, DNA, fingerprints, everything. Does not work on undead, staked vampires, or living beings. The corpse must be relatively fresh (within one week). Extremely useful for Masquerade cleanup.

Level 2: Arms of Ahriman

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Wits + Oblivion | Type: Combat

Summon tendrils of solid shadow from any nearby dark surface. The tendrils can grapple, restrain, or attack targets within 10 meters. Each tendril has Strength equal to your Oblivion rating. You can summon a number of tendrils equal to your successes. Grappled targets must beat the tendril's Strength with their own Strength + Athletics to break free. Tendrils deal 1 Superficial damage per turn to grappled targets. Bright light (spotlight, sunlight) destroys the tendrils instantly. Lasts one scene or until destroyed.

Level 2: Where the Shroud Thins

Cost: Free | Pool: Wits + Oblivion | Type: Utility

Passively sense the presence of ghosts, haunted locations, and areas where the barrier between the living and dead worlds is weak. You feel a cold chill and an emotional echo of the type of death associated with the location. Can also detect Oblivion use by other vampires (Hecata, Lasombra). Range: approximately 50 meters. Does not reveal precise locations — only a general awareness of 'something dead is near.'

Level 3: Touch of Oblivion

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Str + Oblivion | Type: Combat

Channel entropy through your touch, dealing 2 Aggravated damage to a target. The touch causes visible decay — flesh withers, metal rusts, wood rots. Against vampires, the damage manifests as corpse-like decay on the struck area. Against inanimate objects, this can destroy locks, weaken structural supports, or rot through ropes. Requires a successful melee attack (Str + Brawl) to land the touch.

Level 3: Aura of Decay

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Sta + Oblivion | Type: Combat

Emanate an aura of entropy in a 5-meter radius. All organic matter ages and decays — flowers wilt, food rots, wood warps, leather cracks. Living beings (mortals and animals) in the area take 1 Superficial damage per turn and feel intense nausea and despair. Vampires are unaffected physically but feel their Beast stir (+1 difficulty to frenzy checks). Lasts a number of turns equal to successes rolled.

Level 3: Shadow Cast

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Wits + Oblivion | Type: Utility

Animate your own shadow to act independently. The shadow can separate from your body and move up to 20 meters away. It can manipulate light objects (up to 5 lbs), scout around corners, and deliver whispered messages. The shadow has your Wits and Dexterity for relevant rolls but cannot deal damage. If the shadow is 'destroyed' by bright light, you take 1 Superficial Willpower damage. Lasts one scene.

Level 4: Stygian Shroud

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Wits + Oblivion | Type: Combat

Fill a room-sized area (up to 30 meters radius) with impenetrable supernatural darkness. No natural or artificial light functions within — flashlights, fire, and even headlights are snuffed out. Only Oblivion's Sight or Auspex (Heightened Senses) can see through it. Everyone inside without such powers is effectively blind (all visual-based pools reduced to 0). The darkness is cold and oppressive — mortals must roll Courage or flee in panic. Lasts one scene.

Level 4: Necromantic Binding

Cost: 2 Rouse | Pool: Cha + Oblivion vs ghost's Resolve + Awareness | Type: Combat

Bind a ghost to your will. The ghost must be present (use Oblivion's Sight to see it). Contested by the ghost's Resolve + Awareness. On success, the ghost must obey your commands for one lunar cycle. You can command it to haunt a location, spy on people, attack targets (ghosts can manifest to terrify or poltergeist objects), or answer questions about the afterlife. Treat the ghost as having dice pools equal to its pre-death stats. The ghost is resentful and will exploit loopholes in your commands.

Level 5: Withering Spirit

Cost: 2 Rouse | Pool: Res + Oblivion vs Sta + Resolve | Type: Combat

Attack the very essence of a target — their soul or vampiric Beast. The target takes 3 Aggravated Willpower damage (not Health). If this empties their Willpower track, mortals fall into a permanent catatonic state; vampires enter involuntary torpor for a duration determined by their Humanity. The attack is invisible — observers see the target simply collapse. Range: touch. Devastating against enemies you can get close to.

Level 5: Shadowstep

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Wits + Oblivion | Type: Utility

Step into one shadow and emerge from another within line of sight (up to 100 meters). The transition is instant — you dissolve into darkness and reconstitute. You can carry anything you could normally carry. Can be used in combat as movement. The destination shadow must be large enough to contain your body (at least as large as a doorway's shadow). Cannot be used in areas without shadows or under direct bright light. Can be used once per turn.
